programmes.show_description: NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
The challenges and struggles of women whose lives have been affected by factors like war or poverty are chronicled as they attend AUW in Bangladesh to gain education to become new leaders in society.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Using the power of song to overcome natural disaster, young Fukushima survivors of 2011 earthquake performed 'To Bring Happiness,' an anthem of the 1995 Hanshin Earthquake, across Japan.
Soar over the silvery snow-clad expanses of northern Japan where the majestic Japanese Alps rise to greet you.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
A renovation pro revitalises seemingly worthless old buildings and turns them into places full of smiles in the story of an architect who keeps envisioning a warm future in the face of harsh reality.
Featured recipe: Dashimaki Omelette. Learn about Japanese home cooking with Master Chef Saito, who cooks using traditional Japanese cooking techniques, in five minutes!
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Today's theme: Bodies. As AI, digital and virtual spaces take over, thinking about physical bodies is more important than ever - explore the role of designs which are rooted in human physicality.
A selection of acclaimed programmes from NHK's domestic channels dubbed into English.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Worn by samurai on formal occasions, in-ro were palm-sized containers crafted with masterful skill. In each piece, we uncover the refined spirit of samurai dandyism.
The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history and culture - this episode looks at words related to castles. Explore the unique culture behind them.
In September, 11 districts in Yatsuo in Toyama Prefecture parade along different routes performing Etchu Owara-Kaze-no-Bon, a 300-year-old matsuri that features elegant music, dancing, and singing.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
New projects are helping reduce CO2 emissions from construction sites and buildings. Explore reuse of used and surplus materials, a home powered almost 100% by solar, high-tech solar cells and more.

programmes.show_name: Bosai: Science That Can Save Your Life
programmes.show_description: Lava Flows: The hazard map for Mount Fuji was revised in 2021, revealing the chance of more damage from lava flows - we look at what lies behind the assumptions for this assessment.
Aichi / Kataoka Mami (curator). Tokoname, a pottery town with over 900 years of history, creates products appropriate for the times. Kataoka finds the beauty that's present in functional objects.

Takao Michiko devoted 50 years to bridging Japan and Ukraine through ballet. She and her Kyiv-based son drove vital projects despite the war. Defying the sirens, ensuring the curtain never falls...

Second Movement: An autumn visit to Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden, a place where nature from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule of culture and history.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Kajiura Yuki's Anime Universe Comes to tiny desk. Kajiura Yuki,the composer whose music has captivated audiences around the world, arrives at tiny desk concerts JAPAN.The special guest is LiSA.
Discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden in Uda - here the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule as a sanctuary of local culture and history.
Nostalgia is spreading in Japan for the Showa era (1926-1989) - we introduce electronics, retro designs, and other elements of a period that many Japanese people consider the 'good old days'.
Karuizawa is cool even in summer, making it a popular resort town - we meet local cats at a fashionable home goods shop and a restaurant in a remodelled folk home who are sure to bring in customers!
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.

We experience a warm welcome in the hard-working small communities on the islands and coast of the Yamaguchi end of the Seto Inland Sea - they are poor in amenities but rich in happiness and kindness.
Learn programming principles without a computer! Animated characters provide fun and lucid explanations of five core processes: analysis, combination, generalisation, abstraction and simulation.
Among Mee's friends, there's a popular game where you sneak up and squeeze your friends' butts. When someone does it to Mee, Mee shivers and feels weird. Papa says 'That's your Inner Alarm's alert.'
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Philadelphia, the City of Brotherly Love, is the US East Coast's second-largest city after NYC - it's the birthplace of the US and was once the capital, and it's now a favourite for history buffs.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
We head to Nagasaki, the backdrop of the 2024 acclaimed film The Colours Within, with anime singer and super-fan MindaRyn from Thailand. Uncover the reality behind Japan's 'hidden Christian' history.
Miyama is famous for its roof-thatching tradition - the town of Hanase continues to serve its local speciality, foraged wild food cuisine.
Binod-san from Nepal works at a car sales company as a mechanic, but he's not sure what to do when he doesn't understand his superior's instructions in Japanese. He learns how to clarify and confirm.

Trash Tribe is a band led by Oomote Fumiaki whose instruments are made with marine trash. Fishing line and driftwood on strings, plastic bottle percussion - their laid-back music has fans of all ages.
